data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children once more; third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260515T085213-f48326c4/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing child IDs, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a success. Do not mix implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260515T085213-f48326c4/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

`.ddx/executions/20260515T085213-f48326c4/no_changes_rationale.txt` must contain one:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` reject.

To request orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260515T085213-f48326c4/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260515T085213-f48326c4/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and project defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.
  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-ff7a7207">
    <title>work: stop retry churn after pre-execute dirty-tree checkpoint refusal</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
When the project worktree has uncommitted implementation changes, the pre-execute checkpoint correctly refuses to absorb them, but `ddx work --watch` keeps retrying ready beads from the same dirty tree. This produces repeated failed attempt events and gives the operator the appearance of active worker progress even though no actual build can start until the local tree is committed or cleaned.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:567` computes dirty paths before dispatch.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:586` returns `checkpoint refused to absorb implementation changes outside DDx bookkeeping` for non-DDx-bookkeeping dirty paths.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1713` handles that report in watch mode by unclaiming, logging, sleeping, and continuing, so the same project-level blocker recurs across the queue.

PROPOSED FIX
Treat pre-execute dirty-tree checkpoint refusal as a project-level operator attention condition, not an ordinary retryable bead failure. `ddx work` must release the candidate bead, emit one structured event naming the project root and dirty paths, and stop the worker before claiming another bead. `ddx work --watch` must not loop forever on this condition; it should exit or park in an explicit operator-attention terminal result that does not claim additional beads. The stdout/stderr message must tell the operator to commit or clean the listed implementation files before restarting the worker.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go`
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go`
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_checkpoint_test.go`
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_stay_alive_test.go`
- `cli/cmd/work_test.go`

NON-SCOPE
- Do not allow `ddx work` to auto-commit arbitrary implementation files.
- Do not add a hidden force flag that bypasses dirty-tree protection.
- Do not change allowed DDx-bookkeeping checkpoint paths.
- Do not mark the bead itself blocked; the blocker is the local project tree, not the bead content.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent -run 'TestLoop_WatchCheckpointDirtyStopsWithoutRetry|TestLoop_DrainCheckpointDirtyStopsQueue|TestPreDispatchCheckpointRejectsImplementationDirtyPaths' -count=1` passes.
2. `cd cli && go test ./cmd -run 'TestWorkDirtyTreeCheckpointReturnsOperatorAttention' -count=1` passes.
3. The dirty-tree test fixture with two ready beads proves only the first bead is claimed then released; the second bead is never claimed while the same dirty-tree blocker remains.
4. The emitted event/result includes the project root and dirty path list, and the operator-facing message includes `commit or clean` guidance.
5. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>area:agent, area:execution, kind:bug, operator-attention, dirty-tree</labels>
    <metadata spec-id="FEAT-010" base-rev="99c2a2565c96b75a9509ef1bbe8033704f891725" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260515T085213-f48326c4"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <ref id="FEAT-010" path="docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md">Feature: Task Execution</ref>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
Implemented and committed `fix: stop work on checkpoint dirty tree [ddx-ff7a7207]` as `8d9c7cd3d`.

[execute_bead_loop.go](/var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-ff7a7207-20260515T085213-f48326c4/cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go) now treats the pre-execute dirty-tree checkpoint refusal as a terminal operator-attention stop in both drain and watch modes: it unclaims the current bead, emits one `loop.operator_attention` event with `project_root` and `dirty_paths`, returns `StopCondition=OperatorAttention`, and does not retry or claim the next bead. [work.go](/var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-ff7a7207-20260515T085213-f48326c4/cli/cmd/work.go) renders that result with `commit or clean` guidance, and [execute_bead.go](/var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-ff7a7207-20260515T085213-f48326c4/cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go) updates the checkpoint refusal text accordingly.

Verification passed:
- `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent -run 'TestLoop_WatchCheckpointDirtyStopsWithoutRetry|TestLoop_DrainCheckpointDirtyStopsQueue|TestPreDispatchCheckpointRejectsImplementationDirtyPaths' -count=1`
- `cd cli && go test ./cmd -run 'TestWorkDirtyTreeCheckpointReturnsOperatorAttention' -count=1`
- `lefthook run pre-commit` (staged run and commit-time hook run both passed)
